title: TXT Data Packing
text: TXT Data Packing Uniface maps Uniface packing codes in the fixed part of the record to character string versions of the packing code. It writes fields in the variable part of the record in their conceptual format. However, the TXT connector does not support conversion of binary data, so only String-based packing codes (known as Text packing codes) should be used in the variable part of a record. Each packing code is mapped to a Text packing code with a length and format that is most suited to the type of the data to be stored. Search: Column Defined Packing Code Text Packing Code TXT Storage Format Filter results... 29 rows found Table 1. Uniface Packing Codes, Character String Conversions, and TXT Storage Formats Defined Packing Code Text Packing Code TXT Storage Format Filter 29 rows found Filter 29 rows found Filter 29 rows found Defined Packing Code Text Packing Code TXT Storage Format B1-B8 B2 char (format:T/F) C1-C* C1-C* char(1-255) D1-D16 E5 char (format:DD-MMM-YYY HH:NN:SS) E1-E16 E5 char (format: DD-MMM-YYYY HHNN:SS) F4, F8 N20 char(20) G8 N20 char(20) I1 N4 char(4) I2 N6 char(6) I3 N9 char(9) I4 N11 char(11) I8 N21 char(21) J1-J32 J m char( m ) where (0 < m < 33) M1-M8 N20 char(20) N1-N32 N m char( m ) where (0 < m < 33) O1-O31 N( m +1) char( m +1) where (0 < m < 31) O32 N32 char(32) P1 N4 char(4) P2 N6 char(6) P3 N9 char(9) P4, P5 N11 char(11) P6-P16 N20 char(20) Q1 N4 char(4) Q2 N6 char(6) Q3 N9 char(9) Q4, Q5 N11 char(11) Q6-Q16 N20 char(20) T1-T16 E5 char (format: DD-MMM-YYYY HH:NN:SS) VC1-VC* VC1-VC* char(1-*) W1-W* W1-* char(1-*) W Packing Code The W packing code is used for Unicode characters. The TXT connector writes fixed-length W fields as a fixed number of Unicode characters. If a fixed number of bytes is required, define the field as a C field. In this case, you can still use Unicode by specifying it as the default character set using $DEF_CHARSET . Fields in the fixed part of the record that have a W packing code are provided to the connector in UTF32 encoding. All other fields in the fixed part of the record are provided in the default character set specified by $DEF_CHARSET . Fields in the variable part of the record that have a W packing code are provided to the connector in UTF8 format, and other fields are provided in their conceptual format. String fields are provided in $DEF_CHARSET . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
